user_query:
I need a deterministic pre-show lineup builder for tonight’s newscast. Use the files under input/ and produce three structured outputs under output/.

Inputs:
- input/headlines.jsonl: newline-delimited JSON objects with fields: id, title, source, topic, region, timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC), priority (high|normal|low), embargo_until (ISO 8601 UTC).
- input/audience_weights.csv: columns topic,weight giving our current audience affinity by topic (0.0–1.0).
- input/reference.yaml: has a single key reference_time (ISO 8601 UTC) to use as “now.”

Task:
1) Eligibility and de-duplication:
- Consider only records where timestamp <= reference_time AND embargo_until <= reference_time.
- Deduplicate by title, case-insensitive and trimmed. Keep one record per title: the one with the most recent timestamp; if timestamps tie, prefer higher priority (high > normal > low); if still tied, choose the lexicographically smallest source.

2) Scoring (for each eligible, de-duplicated story):
- hours_since = (reference_time - timestamp) in hours as a floating value.
- recency_score = max(0, 1 - hours_since/48).
- priority_boost: map priority high=1.0, normal=0.5, low=0.0.
- audience_weight: lookup by topic from input/audience_weights.csv (if a topic is missing, use 0.5).
- lineup_score = 0.6*audience_weight + 0.3*priority_boost + 0.1*recency_score.

3) Outputs:
- output/eligible_stories.csv: all eligible, de-duplicated stories with columns [id, title, source, topic, region, timestamp, embargo_until, priority, hours_since, recency_score, audience_weight, priority_boost, lineup_score], sorted by lineup_score desc then timestamp desc.
- output/top_lineup.csv: the top 10 stories for tonight after enforcing a per-topic cap of 3. Ranking: sort eligible stories by lineup_score desc; break ties by recency_score desc, then title asc; as you traverse in rank order, include a story only if its topic has fewer than 3 already selected. Output columns [rank (1..N), id, title, topic, source, lineup_score, recency_score, priority, region].
- output/summary_by_topic.csv: for every topic present in eligible_stories.csv, produce one row with columns [topic, eligible_count, selected_count, avg_lineup_score, avg_title_length, most_common_source]. avg_lineup_score is the mean over all eligible stories in that topic; avg_title_length is the average number of characters in title as-is; most_common_source is the source with the highest count among eligible for that topic (break ties alphabetically).

Notes:
- All three outputs are required and must be written to the specified paths.
- Use reference_time from input/reference.yaml for all time calculations.
- Ensure de-duplication and the per-topic cap are applied exactly as described so our team can validate the selections quickly. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
